task_completion.description: Create the custom Wazuh decoder named 'bastion-session' matching the program name 'session_auth'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or Wazuh UI showing XML configuration with <decoder name="bastion-session"> and <program_name>session_auth</program_name>.

task_completion.description: Configure the decoder to extract user, srcip, action, status, and extra_data fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: The decoder XML configuration shows a <regex> or similar extraction tag, along with an <order> tag specifying 'user, srcip, action, status, extra_data' (mapping src_ip to srcip and reason to extra_data).

XML configuration showing <rule id="100010" level="8">, a condition matching the status 'failed' (e.g., <field name="status">failed</field>), and <description>Bastion host failed authentication</description>.

XML configuration showing <rule id="100011" level="3">, a condition matching the status 'success' (e.g., <field name="status">success</field>), and <description>Bastion host successful authentication</description>.

task_completion.description: Apply and verify the configuration using wazuh-logtest or by restarting the manager
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest' with a pasted log line successfully triggering rule 100010 or 100011, OR a terminal showing a successful restart of the wazuh-manager service.

integrity.description: Verify the agent edited the actual Wazuh configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths in the text editor should be within '/var/ossec/etc/decoders/' and '/var/ossec/etc/rules/' (e.g., local_decoder.xml and local_rules.xml), or the official Wazuh web UI should be used, rather than a random standalone file.

integrity.description: Verify the wazuh-logtest tool was genuinely executed
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the interactive prompt for wazuh-logtest and the standard multi-phase output (Phase 1: log, Phase 2: decoder, Phase 3: rule), not just a printed or echoed string.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not mock the verification results
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no 'echo' commands used to print fake logtest results or fake systemctl success messages to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom decoder must be named 'bastion-session' and filter on the program name 'session_auth'. The failed login rule must use ID '100010' and the successful login rule must use ID '100011'. A valid test log line for verification is: 'Jan 15 10:25:01 bastion-gw session_auth[5236]: user=root src_ip=203.0.113.42 action=login status=failed reason="account_locked"'.
